milestones.vision: Transform the game from emoji placeholders to a fully illustrated, themed experience — AI-generated animal art, multiple visual themes with real backgrounds and card backs, richer animations, atmospheric sound, and the kind of tactile feel that makes a toddler want to keep tapping.
success_criteria: All card faces show AI-generated illustrated animals instead of emoji
At least 2 selectable visual themes with distinct AI-generated backgrounds, card backs, and color palettes
All card interactions (flip, match, mismatch) have layered animations with appropriate easing and timing
Background ambient sound and improved SFX with mute toggle
Screen transitions are smooth and intentional — no jarring cuts
Game feels responsive and tactile on iPad (primary target device)
PWA service worker cache updated for new image assets
Total asset payload stays under 2MB for fast loading

key_risks.risk: Image generation consistency across a set of animals
key_risks.whyItMatters: Each card needs a distinct animal in the same art style. Imagen prompt engineering must produce cohesive sets, not random styles per image.
16 animal illustrations + theme backgrounds + card backs could bloat the app. Need to compress aggressively and consider lazy loading.
CSS 3D transforms on image-heavy cards may perform differently than emoji text nodes. Need to verify on iPad.

key_risks.risk: Web Audio ambient sound may not auto-play on iOS
key_risks.whyItMatters: iOS requires user gesture before audio context can play. Background music needs careful activation UX.

key_risks.risk: Theme switching complexity in vanilla CSS
key_risks.whyItMatters: Themes now include swapping images (not just colors), so theme switching needs to preload assets to avoid flash of broken images.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A full set of 16 animal illustrations generated with consistent style, verified visually
Total compressed image payload measured and kept under budget
Card flip animations with image faces run at 60fps — verified in browser dev tools
Audio plays correctly after first user tap, mute toggle works, no console errors
Switching themes loads new assets without visible flash or layout shift

milestones.verification_contract: Each slice verified by serving locally and testing in browser. Images checked for visual consistency, animations for smoothness, themes for completeness, audio for correct behavior.
milestones.verification_integration: After all slices: full playthrough on each theme, all difficulty levels, with sound on/off, verifying no regressions and acceptable load times.
milestones.verification_operational: PWA cache version bumped, service worker updated with new asset list, offline mode tested with all theme assets cached.
milestones.verification_uat: Play the game start to finish on each theme — it should feel polished, illustrated, and fun. Cards should look like a real children's game, not a tech demo.
definition_of_done: All 16 animal card illustrations generated and compressed
2+ complete themes with generated backgrounds and card backs

Service worker cache updated with all new assets

# S01: Asset Generation & Pipeline

**Generated and compressed a full illustrated asset set — 16 animals + 2 themes — totaling 232KB in WebP.**

## What Happened

Generated a complete illustrated asset set for the game using Imagen 4 Fast API. Established a kawaii flat-vector prompt template that produces consistent style across all 16 animals. Generated theme-specific card backs and backgrounds for default (purple/magical) and jungle (green/tropical) themes. Compressed everything from ~12MB raw PNGs to 232KB WebP — well under the 1.5MB budget.

## Verification

All 20 WebP assets present. Total size 232KB (budget 1.5MB). Visual inspection confirms consistent style and quality at display dimensions.

## Requirements Advanced

None.

## Requirements Validated

None.

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Deviations

Hit Imagen 4 rate limit (10 req/min) during animal generation. Added retry logic with backoff to the generator script.

## Known Limitations

Imagen 4 rate limit is 10 req/min on free tier — regenerating assets requires patience.

## Follow-ups

None.

## Files Created/Modified

- `assets/animals/` — 16 kawaii animal illustrations (WebP, 256px)
- `assets/themes/default/` — Default theme card back + background (WebP)
- `assets/themes/jungle/` — Jungle theme card back + background (WebP)
- `tools/generate-image.js` — Image generation utility script
- `tools/generate-all-animals.js` — Batch animal generator with retry logic

decisions.decision: How to generate game assets (images and audio)
decisions.choice: Imagen 4 Fast API for image generation ($0.02/image), Web Audio synthesis for SFX, with generate-image.js utility script in tools/
decisions.rationale: Imagen 4 Fast delivers high-quality flat illustrations at the lowest API cost. Gemini audio models are TTS-focused (speech only), not suitable for game sound effects. Web Audio synthesis is instant, zero-download, and already proven in the existing codebase. The utility script loads GEMINI_API_KEY from .env.

decisions.choice: Direct CDN URLs from PokeAPI/sprites GitHub repo
decisions.rationale: User specified the exact URL pattern. No API calls needed — just construct https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/{id}.png directly. Zero-auth, fast, canonical source.

decisions.choice: Random selection from 1-151 per game start, not app load
decisions.rationale: User emphasized replayability — different Pokémon each round. Selection happens in startPokemonGame(), not global scope, so each new game gets a fresh random set.

decisions.decision: Image loading strategy for Pokémon level
decisions.choice: Wait for all images first, then reveal board
decisions.rationale: User chose 'Wait for all images first' option. Clean reveal once everything is ready, brief loading spinner before the board appears. Prevents partially-rendered states and broken image icons.

decisions.choice: Skip and draw a replacement from the pool
decisions.rationale: User chose 'Skip and draw a replacement' option. Game always shows 8 working pairs by validating each sprite load and retrying with new IDs until 8 valid pairs exist. Retry limit prevents infinite loops if network is completely down.
User chose 'Stand out — unique color/icon' option. Makes it clear this is a different kind of level, more exciting for the user's son to discover. Should still fit the overall design language.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if user feedback suggests it's too visually noisy
